chunks.text: Borivoje Nikolic is a Professor in the Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley. His contact information includes office at 509 Cory Hall, phone 510-643-9297, fax 642-2845, email bora@eecs.berkeley.edu, and office hours Monday 10-11am. He also has a research assistant Esther Gomez with contact titagomez@berkeley.edu.

Prof. Nikolic received his Dipl.Ing. and M.Sc. in electrical engineering from University of Belgrade, Serbia in 1992 and 1994, and Ph.D. from UC Davis in 1999. He lectured at University of Belgrade from 1992-1996, worked at Texas Instruments Storage Products Group, and joined UC Berkeley in 1999. His research includes digital/analog IC design and VLSI implementation of communications/signal processing algorithms. He co-authored the book Digital Integrated Circuits: A Design Perspective, 2nd edition in 2003.

Dr. Nikolic received numerous awards including NSF CAREER award in 2003, Best Doctoral Dissertation prizes at UC Davis in 1999, and Belgrade Award for Best Diploma Thesis in 1992. He and colleagues have received best paper awards at IEEE International Solid-State Circuits Conference, Symposium on VLSI Circuits, IEEE International SOI Conference, European Solid-State Device Research Conference, European Solid-State Circuits Conference, IEEE Custom Integrated Circuits Conference, S3S Conference, Design Automation Conference, and ACM/IEEE International Symposium of Low-Power Electronics.

From 2014-2015, Nikolic was a Distinguished Lecturer of IEEE Solid-State Circuits Society. He served as Technical Program Chair for 2022 Symposium on VLSI Technology and Circuits and General Chair for 2024 IEEE Symposium on VLSI Technology and Circuits. His education includes Ph.D. in Electrical and Computer Engineering from UC Davis (1999), M.Sc. in Electrical Engineering from University of Belgrade (1994), and Dipl.Ing. in Electrical Engineering from University of Belgrade (1992).

Nikolic's research areas include Integrated Circuits (INC), Computer Architecture & Engineering (ARC), Information, Data, Network, and Communication Sciences (IDNCS), and Cyber-Physical Systems and Design Automation (CPSDA). He is affiliated with Berkeley Wireless Research Center (BWRC) and SpeciaLIzed Computing Ecosystems (SLICE). His teaching includes courses EECS 151, EECS 151LA, EECS 151LB, EECS 251A, EECS 251LA, and EECS 251LB on digital design and integrated circuits.

Selected publications include works on self-adjustable clock generators, CMOS transceivers, adaptive voltage scaling, I-V variability characterization, SRAM stability, LDPC decoders, pipeline ADCs, and energy-performance optimization methods. Notable is the book Digital Integrated Circuits: A Design Perspective co-authored with Rabaey and Chandrakasan. Publications span IEEE journals and conferences from 2003-2016.

Selected honors and awards include SSCS Innovative Education Award (2024), IEEE Fellow (2017), IEEE Electron Devices Society Fellow (2017), SSCS Distinguished Lecturer (2014), SSCC ISSCC Jack Kilby Award for Outstanding Student Paper (2004), and NSF Faculty Early Career Development Award (CAREER) (2003). These recognize contributions to solid-state circuits and education.
